title: A tool for 3D representation of the 2d thermographic breast acquisitions
author: Moura, Eudoxia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This study enhances the three-dimensional reconstruction of the breast surface from two-dimensional thermal images at five different angles. The process involves the use of a convolutional neural network U-net for image segmentation, extraction of relevant points and curves, geometric transformations to add a dimension to the curves, and modeling with Rational Non-uniform B-splines Surface (NURBS). The work aims to improve the previously proposed method by making modifications to the rotation points and cutting regions to achieve a more comprehensive breast contour. In a quantitative evaluation of the results, comparing FLIR acquisitions (thermal images) and Scanner with the old and new methods, we observed that, concerning scanning, the old method has an average of 37.07% with a standard deviation of 6.27, while the new method exhibits an average of 14.06% with a standard deviation of 7.28. When compared to FLIR images, the old method has an average of 37.47% with a standard deviation of 7.24, whereas the new method shows an average of 14.77% with a standard deviation of 7.37. These results indicate that the new method significantly improves the representation of the breast shape compared to the old methods.  
   Keywords:   3D Breast Models   -   Thermography Images   -   Edge Detection
